notes: Avoid reusing endless-runner framing or glossy sci-fi poster language that would make the low-detail shipped build feel misrepresented.
Keep the listing honest to the real floating-bridge layout, the amber/cobalt bridge-state rule, and the small purple-and-white runner silhouette.

aiExploration.prompt: Create three distinct marketing poster concepts for an original game called Shardspan. The game is a desktop-only 3D relay sprint set over a deep blue void with floating amber bridges, cobalt bridges, black relay pedestals, white crystal shards, a tiny purple-and-white robot runner, and sparse floating islands. Show the title SHARDSPAN clearly in the central composition. Keep the art honest to the shipped game: low-detail geometry, bold color blocking, and simple shapes, not a hyper-detailed sci-fi movie poster. Explore three directions: one close relay-and-runner action poster, one wide course poster, and one abstract shard-and-bridge poster.
aiExploration.exportNote: Direct thumbnail fetches from the returned Canva URLs resolved to HTML wrappers in this environment, so the AI batch stayed at concept-exploration level only and was not used as final media.

finalFamily.approach: Hand-authored HTML/CSS composition rendered through Playwright from real Shardspan capture frames.
whyItWon: The final family guarantees that the title, palette, relay crystal silhouette, and amber-versus-cobalt bridge language all match the shipped build exactly.
The final icon is full-bleed square art, not a badge on a matte, and it keeps the shard-and-span motif readable at small size.
The final heroes stay visually stronger than raw screenshots without inventing detail that the live build does not actually ship.
